rent.note: furnished 1BR in city center; avg $240 center / $213 outside; $400-500 for comfortable modern unit

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; basic utilities avg $68/mo for 85sqm; internet extra

groceries.note: Lee Plaza + Robinsons + local markets; Philippines food generally affordable

diningOut.note: boulevard food stalls, local carinderias ($1-2/meal), some expat-oriented restaurants

transit.note: tricycles $0.25-0.50; monthly bus pass $8; very cheap local transport

coworking.note: limited coworking spaces; student city + professionals; cafes serve as workspace

simData.note: Globe/Smart unlimited data; Philippines SIM rates $10-15/mo

misc.note: diving, island day trips, gym, personal care, entertainment; slow-paced city

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers applied to RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Raise for peak tourist/weather months, lower for off-peak.

evidence.claim: Dumaguete 1BR city center avg $240; $553/mo without rent; total ~$793/mo
evidence.quote: the average monthly rent for a 1-bedroom apartment in the city centre is $240. A single person needs $553 per month without rent

evidence.claim: Dumaguete comfortable housing $400-500/month; total realistic $1,500-1,800/mo for comfort
evidence.quote: If you can manage a housing budget of $400 to $500, you will have no difficulty finding a modern and comfortable home. Dumaguete or provincial areas can cost $1,500 to $1,800 monthly

evidence.claim: Dumaguete basic utilities avg $68/mo for 85sqm; monthly transport pass $8
evidence.quote: Basic utilities (including electricity, heating, cooling, water, and garbage for an 85m² apartment) cost an average of $68 per month. A monthly pass is typically priced at $8

evidence.claim: Philippines DNV launched June 2025; requires $24,000/year remote income; up to 2 years
evidence.quote: The Philippines launched a dedicated Digital Nomad Visa in June 2025 requiring $24,000/year remote income, and the Digital Nomad Visa enables up to two years legally, tax-free on foreign earnings
